title: Ghanteswara Siva Temple
tagline: Ghanteswara Siva Temple — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Ghanteswara Siva Temple is located in the Kapileswara precinct.
overview.description: Ghanteswara Siva Temple is located in the Kapileswara precinct. The enshrined deity is a Siva-lingam over a square yonipitha made of laterite. The temple has a vimana in pidha order. It is triratha on plan.

topPlaces.name: Laxmi Narayan Temple
topPlaces.description: Laxmi Narayan Temple is located in the Kapileswar temple precinct. The enshrined deity is Laxmi- Narayana seated in padmasana over a lotus pedestal.

topPlaces.name: Somabara Mandapa (Vishnu Temple)
topPlaces.description: Vishnu Temple is located within the Kapilesvara Temple precinct Kapilesvara village, Old Town, Bhubaneswar. It was built in the 15th century AD. The temple is facing towards east and the presiding deities of this temple are two Vishnu images, and the image of Jagannatha, Balabhadra, Subhadra and Buddha.

topPlaces.name: Siddhesvara Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Siddhesvara Siva Temple is located inside the Kapilesvara temple precinct, Kapilesvara village, Old Town, Bhubaneswar. It was built in the 15th century AD. The temple is facing east and the presiding deity of the temple is a Siva lingam within a circular yonipitha, which is made of laterite.

topPlaces.name: Somabaresvara Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: The Somabaresvara Siva Temple is located in the Kapilesvara temple precinct (Lat- 200 15’ 53" N., Long- 850 51’ 40" E., Elev- 56 ft). The enshrined deity is a Sivalingam over a square yonipitha made of sandstone.

topPlaces.name: Visvanath Siva Temple, Bhubaneswar
topPlaces.description: Visvanath Siva temple is situated in the Kotitirthesvara temple precinct in Bhubaneswar, Orissa, India.

topPlaces.name: Champakesvara Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Champakeswara Shiva Temple is located Ambika Sahi in the Old Town area of Bhubaneswar in Odisha, India. It is 157 metres west of Parasuramesvara on the right side of the Kotitirthesvara lane leading to Bindu sagara.

topPlaces.name: Patalesvara Siva Temple – I
topPlaces.description: Patalesvara Siva Temple – I is a temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located in Old Town, Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India. This temple dates back to 13th century, and belongs to the Ganga era.

topPlaces.name: Kotitirtha Tank
topPlaces.description: Kotitirtha Tank, also known as Kotitirthesar Tank, is a sacred tank, used as a ritual bath in Hinduism, located near the Kotitirthesvara temple in the Kotitirthesvara lane, Old Town, Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India. It is now under the care and maintenance of the Kotitrithesvara Thakura Development Committee.

items.note: Laxmi Narayan Temple is located in the Kapileswar temple precinct. The enshrined deity is Laxmi- Narayana seated in padmasana over a lotus pedestal.

Vishnu Temple is located within the Kapilesvara Temple precinct Kapilesvara village, Old Town, Bhubaneswar. It was built in the 15th century AD. The temple is facing towards east and the presiding deities of this temple are two Vishnu images, and the image of Jagannatha, Balabhadra, Subhadra and Buddha.

Siddhesvara Siva Temple is located inside the Kapilesvara temple precinct, Kapilesvara village, Old Town, Bhubaneswar. It was built in the 15th century AD. The temple is facing east and the presiding deity of the temple is a Siva lingam within a circular yonipitha, which is made of laterite.

The Somabaresvara Siva Temple is located in the Kapilesvara temple precinct (Lat- 200 15’ 53" N., Long- 850 51’ 40" E., Elev- 56 ft). The enshrined deity is a Sivalingam over a square yonipitha made of sandstone.
Visvanath Siva temple is situated in the Kotitirthesvara temple precinct in Bhubaneswar, Orissa, India.

Champakeswara Shiva Temple is located Ambika Sahi in the Old Town area of Bhubaneswar in Odisha, India. It is 157 metres west of Parasuramesvara on the right side of the Kotitirthesvara lane leading to Bindu sagara.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Patalesvara Siva Temple – I & around

items.note: Patalesvara Siva Temple – I is a temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located in Old Town, Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India. This temple dates back to 13th century, and belongs to the Ganga era.

Kotitirtha Tank, also known as Kotitirthesar Tank, is a sacred tank, used as a ritual bath in Hinduism, located near the Kotitirthesvara temple in the Kotitirthesvara lane, Old Town, Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India. It is now under the care and maintenance of the Kotitrithesvara Thakura Development Committee.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ghanteswara Siva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ghanteswara Siva Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ghanteswara Siva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (5 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ghanteswara Siva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ghanteswara Siva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ghanteswara Siva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Ghanteswara Siva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ghanteswara Siva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Laxmi Narayan Temple, Somabara Mandapa (Vishnu Temple), Siddhesvara Siva Temple, Somabaresvara Siva Temple, Visvanath Siva Temple, Bhubaneswar.
